message.content:
Look at these files to understand how navigation links are rendered in the sidebar:
1. client/src/components/SideBar/Sidebar.jsx - how it renders the `links` prop
2. client/src/components/HomePage/home.css - any existing styles for sidebar links, freedom-wall, or CTA buttons
3. client/src/components/mobileNavLink/MobileNavLink.jsx - how mobile nav renders links

I need to understand the structure so I can make the "Freedom Wall" link stand out as a CTA button. Return the relevant rendering code and CSS.

content.text: I'll search for those files and analyze how navigation links are rendered, particularly looking at the styling and structure.
Now let me search for the sidebar CSS to see how links are styled:

Perfect! Now I have all the information you need. Let me provide a comprehensive summary of how navigation links are rendered and styled. ## Navigation Link Rendering & Styling Analysis ### 1. **Sidebar Link Rendering (Sidebar.jsx)** The sidebar renders links from a `links` prop array. Each link object can have: - `label` - Display text - `path` - Route path for comparison - `icon` - React component/element - `action` - Click handler function - `className` - Optional custom class (overrides default) - `notifCount` - Optional notification badge **Rendering Code (lines 29-42):** ```jsx {links.map((link, index) => { return(
{link.icon} {link.notifCount && (
{link.notifCount}
)}
{link.label}
) })} ``` **Key Point:** The link uses a custom `className` if provided, otherwise defaults to `sidebar-links-active` (if current path matches) or `sidebar-links`. ### 2. **Sidebar CSS Styling (sidebar.css)** **Regular Link (.sidebar-links):** - Padding: `0.6rem 0.8rem 0.6rem 1rem` - Gap: `0.85rem` - Border radius: `12px` - Font size: `0.95rem` - Text color: `var(--text-secondary)` (muted) - Hover: Applies `var(--bg-hover)` background **Active Link (.sidebar-links-active):** - Same padding, gap, border-radius, font size - Text color: `var(--text-primary)` (bright) - Font weight: `720` (bolder) - Hover: Also applies `var(--bg-hover)` background **CTA Button Example (.write-journal-bttn):** - Padding: `0.6rem 0` - Margin top: `0.3rem` - Border radius: `12px` - Background: `var(--accent-dark)` (solid color, not text) - Text color: `rgb(255, 255, 255)` (white) - Font weight: `650` - Hover: Uses `var(--accent-dark-hover)` (darker shade) - Transition: `0.15s ease` ### 3. **Mobile Navigation Rendering (MobileNavLink.jsx)** Mobile nav is defined as a hardcoded array in the component (lines 26-108) with icon properties. Each mobile nav item renders: ```jsx
link.action(link.path)}>
{link.icon(isActive)} {link.notifCount && (
{link.notifCount}
)}

{link.label}

{isActive &&
}
``` ### 4. **Mobile Navigation CSS (mobilenavlink.css)** **Mobile Nav Container:** Fixed bottom bar (line 17-35) - Position: Fixed bottom `0.6rem` - Background: `var(--bg-card)` with `blur(20px)` - Border radius: `16px` - Box shadow: `0 4px 20px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08)` **Mobile Nav Item (.mobile-navlink-item):** - Flex column layout - Border radius: `12px` - Transition: `background-color 0.2s ease` - Active state: `background-color: var(--bg-pill)` **Mobile Label Colors:** - Inactive: `var(--text-faint, #999)` - Active: `#5f92ff` (blue accent) --- ## How to Make "Freedom Wall" Stand Out as CTA **Option 1: Custom className (Recommended)** Pass a custom `className` in the link object to completely override default styles: ```javascript { label: 'Freedom Wall', path: '/freedom-wall', action: () => navigate('/freedom-wall'), icon: , className: 'sidebar-cta-button' // Custom class overrides defaults } ``` **Option 2: Use the write-journal-bttn pattern** The existing `.write-journal-bttn` class already has CTA styling you can reference. It uses: - Solid background color (`var(--accent-dark)`) - White text - Hover state with darker variant - Margin/padding for prominence **File Paths:** - `/absolute/path/C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SideBar/Sidebar.jsx` - `/absolute/path/C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/SideBar/sidebar.css` - `/absolute/path/C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mobileNavLink/MobileNavLink.jsx` - `/absolute/path/C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/mobileNavLink/mobilenavlink.css`
